perturbation.original_text: B. In addition to the foregoing, and subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable content agreement with such Licensor, COMPANY agrees to pay directly to each Licensor from whom CONTENT PROVIDER might obtain Content which CONTENT PROVIDER licenses to COMPANY pursuant hereto a royalty equal to that royalty which CONTENT PROVIDER might be obligated to pay to that Licensor with respect to the use and exploitation of that Content in the manner licensed to and actually used by COMPANY pursuant hereto provided, however, unless the Parties might agree in writing to the contrary, in no event will COMPANY be obligated to pay such Licensor for the use of such Content more than fifty percent (50.0%) of all revenues generated during the Term of this Agreement from banner advertising that appears on Web site pages that display that Content or any portion thereof and with respect to which at least a majority of the content (excluding advertisements) on such pages is composed of the Content (the "Net Advertising Revenue").
perturbation.changed_text: B. In addition to the foregoing, and subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable content agreement with such Licensor, COMPANY agrees to pay directly to each Licensor from whom CONTENT PROVIDER might obtain Content which CONTENT PROVIDER licenses to COMPANY pursuant hereto a royalty equal to that royalty which CONTENT PROVIDER might be obligated to pay to that Licensor with respect to the use and exploitation of that Content in the manner licensed to and actually used by COMPANY pursuant hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause limiting COMPANY's royalty payment obligation to 50% of Net Advertising Revenue, this creates an in-text contradiction because the subsequent sections (2.C, 2.D, 2.E) still refer to the "Net Advertising Revenue" calculation and reporting. With this clause removed, the extent of Company's obligation and how that should be calculate is undefined, creating uncertainty and ambiguity in enforcing the payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. Subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable content agreement with such Licensor, the Net Advertising Revenue shall be calculated on a quarterly calendar basis (the "Net Advertising Revenue Period") and shall be payable no later than sixty (60) days after the termination of the preceding full quarter-annual period, i.e., commencing on the first (1st) day of January, April, July and October except that the first and last calendar periods may be "short," depending on the effective date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the COMPANY's royalty payment obligation to 50% of Net Advertising Revenue. The changed text removes this limitation. The contradicted text (Section C) refers to the 'Net Advertising Revenue' calculation. Because the changed text removes the 50% cap tied to Net Advertising Revenue while Section C still references it, this creates an in-text contradiction. The calculation of payment is undefined, which makes section C impossible to enforce.

perturbation.original_text: A. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, CONTENT PROVIDER hereby grants and assigns by means of present assignment to COMPANY and COMPANY hereby assumes for the Term of this Agreement (as set forth in paragraph 8, below), CONTENT PROVIDER'S rights and obligations regarding the Content from Licensors as set forth in Exhibit A with respect to the right and license for the territory of the People Republic of China to use, reproduce, distribute, transmit and publicly display the Current Content and the Future Content by means of the internet in accordance with Exhibit A and this Agreement. In this regard, it is specifically understood and agreed that CONTENT PROVIDER will not during the Term of this Agreement take any action to exploit or otherwise use, reproduce, distribute, transmit and publicly display any of the Content via the internet to Universities and College students in the People's Republic of China except for the benefit of the COMPANY.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, CONTENT PROVIDER hereby grants and assigns by means of present assignment to COMPANY and COMPANY hereby assumes for the Term of this Agreement (as set forth in paragraph 8, below), CONTENT PROVIDER'S rights and obligations regarding the Content from Licensors as set forth in Exhibit A with respect to the right and license for the territory of the People Republic of China to use, reproduce, distribute, transmit and publicly display the Current Content and the Future Content by means of the internet in accordance with Exhibit A and this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause preventing CONTENT PROVIDER from exploiting the Content via the internet to Universities and College students in the People's Republic of China except for the benefit of the COMPANY, creates an in-text contradiction. Later in the contract, in Section 5.A, it is stated that "CONTENT PROVIDER will have sole control and responsibility over the data and information contained in the Content." With the omission of the clause of Universities, it gives a sense of contradiction in the section 5.A, since the CONTENT PROVIDER, will have to be constantly verifying if they are or aren't exploiting the content on Universities, which gives a responsibility on COMPANY.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable content agreement with such Licensor, CONTENT PROVIDER will have sole control and responsibility over the data and information contained in the Content.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly restricted CONTENT PROVIDER from exploiting the Content via the internet to Universities and College students in the People's Republic of China except for the benefit of the COMPANY. The changed text removes this restriction. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text (A. Subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable content agreement with such Licensor, CONTENT PROVIDER will have sole control and responsibility over the data and information contained in the Content.), because without the restriction, CONTENT PROVIDER could exploit the data in ways that undermine the 'sole control and responsibility' clause, making it unclear how to reconcile these potentially competing rights. This creates an in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition provided, specifically that 'essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.'
